title: AH-Verify Scanner Data Contract
description: Defines the exact payload shape the Endpoint Scanner must send to the AH-Verify ingestion endpoint. Every field listed here is required unless explicitly marked otherwise. If a field is not requested in this contract, it will not be accepted.

scanMeta.description: Metadata about the scan itself. Sent once per payload.

scanId.description: Unique identifier for this scan run.

scannedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the scan completed.

scannerVersion.description: Semantic version of the scanner binary (e.g. '0.3.1').

scanDurationMs.description: Wall-clock duration of the scan in milliseconds.

scanRoots.description: Absolute directory paths the scanner walked. Tells the dashboard what scope the results cover.

prompts.description: Every prompt template discovered on the endpoint. Each entry MUST have a globally-unique 'id'.

id.description: Globally unique identifier for this prompt. Use '<sourceFilePath>:<startLine>' or a deterministic UUID derived from file path + content hash. Must be stable across scans of the same file with the same content.

name.description: Human-readable display name. Derive from filename, front-matter title, or containing folder. MUST NOT be a generic label like 'agents' — if the source file is 'agents.md', use the parent directory or a distinguishing qualifier (e.g. 'zeroclaw/agents', 'ah-verify/agents'). When multiple prompts share a source filename, prepend the project or parent directory name.

sourceFilePath.description: Absolute path to the file where the prompt was found (e.g. '/Users/will/project/.github/copilot-instructions.md'). This is what the dashboard uses to distinguish items that would otherwise share a name.

tokens.description: Token count of the prompt body (using the model's tokenizer or cl100k_base).

contentHash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the raw prompt body. Used for change detection across scans.

lastChangedDate.description: ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) when the prompt file was last modified on disk.

capabilities.description: Capabilities the prompt grants or implies when executed.

secretRefs.description: Credential material found or referenced inside the prompt body.

label.description: Short descriptor (e.g. 'Env var reference (safe)' or 'Hardcoded token found').

detail.description: The literal reference or redacted value (e.g. '$OPENAI_API_KEY' or 'sk-proj-****...a4f2').

tone.description: 'safe' for env-var refs. 'danger' for inline/hardcoded secrets.

injectionSurfaces.description: Prompt injection attack surfaces detected via static analysis.

dependencies.description: External services, models, or tools this prompt depends on at runtime.

riskScore.description: Composite risk score. 0 = no risk, 100 = critical.

skills.description: Every reusable skill/tool discovered on the endpoint.

id.description: Globally unique identifier for this skill. Use the skill name if unique, or '<name>-<hash>' if collisions exist.

name.description: Display name for this skill (e.g. 'python', 'filesystem', 'shell'). May share a name if two different configs define the same capability — use 'id' for dedup.

description.description: One-line summary of what the skill does. Must be specific — not just 'python capability' but e.g. 'Executes Python scripts via local interpreter with unrestricted filesystem access'.

name.description: Permission label (e.g. 'Filesystem read/write', 'Network access', 'Shell execution', 'Database access', 'Secrets access').

required.description: Whether this permission is actively used by the skill.

apis.description: External API services the skill calls (e.g. 'GitHub API', 'npm Registry'). Do NOT put documentation URLs here — only runtime service dependencies.

consumers.description: Agents and apps that invoke this skill. Each consumer entry MUST reference the agent or app by its unique 'id', not by a generic name.

id.description: The unique 'id' of the consuming agent or app (matching agents[].id or agenticApps[].id).

name.description: Display name of the consuming agent or app.

type.description: Whether the consumer is an agent or an agentic app.

invocations.description: Total invocation count observed during the scan window. Use 0 only if invocation tracking is not available — do not fabricate counts.

mcpServers.description: Every MCP server instance discovered on the endpoint.

id.description: Globally unique identifier for this MCP server. Use the package identifier (e.g. 'io.github.github/github-mcp-server') or '<name>-<hash>' if needed.

name.description: Display name for the server (e.g. 'github-mcp-server', 'playwright-mcp'). Use the short package name, not the full reverse-DNS identifier.

auth.description: Authentication mechanism protecting the server surface.

verified.description: Whether the server's identity and integrity have been cryptographically verified.

command.description: The shell command used to launch this MCP server (e.g. 'npx -y @anthropic/mcp-server-github'). Useful for audit trail and identifying supply chain risk.

tools.description: Every tool the MCP server exposes. The scanner MUST enumerate individual tools from the server's tool manifest — do NOT emit a single generic '<server>_invoke' entry. If tool enumeration fails, set this to an empty array and note the failure in the tool description.

name.description: Actual tool function name as declared by the server (e.g. 'create_issue', 'run_shell_command', 'browser_navigate'). NOT a synthetic '<server>_invoke' stub.

risk.description: Assessed risk level based on capability scope.

description.description: One-line summary of what the tool does. Pull from the server's tool schema description field.

dependentAgents.description: IDs of agents that reference or invoke tools on this server. These MUST be cross-referenced from the agents[].tools array — if an agent lists a tool of type 'mcp' whose name matches this server, include that agent's id here.

agents.description: Every AI agent definition discovered on the endpoint. Each entry MUST have a globally-unique 'id'.

id.description: Globally unique identifier for this agent. Use '<sourceFilePath>:<startLine>' or a deterministic UUID derived from content. Must be stable across scans of the same file with the same content.

name.description: Human-readable display name. Derive from the filename, front-matter title, 'name' field in the agent config, or containing project directory. MUST NOT be a generic label like 'agents'. When the source file is 'AGENTS.md', use the project directory as a prefix (e.g. 'zeroclaw/agents', 'ah-verify/agents') or extract the agent's declared name from the file's content.

sourceFilePath.description: Absolute path to the file where the agent definition was found.

executionModel.description: How the agent is triggered and supervised.

trustScore.description: Composite trust score. 0 = untrusted, 100 = fully trusted.

version.description: Semantic version of the agent definition (e.g. 'v2.1.0'). Use 'unknown' only if genuinely undetectable.

author.description: Team or individual who authored the agent. Check git blame, package.json author, or file metadata. Use 'unknown' only if genuinely undetectable.

sourceRepo.description: Repository URL or local path where the agent source lives. Check nearest .git/config for remote origin. Use 'unknown' only if genuinely undetectable.

capabilities.description: System capabilities the agent has access to.

enabled.description: Whether this capability is active for the agent.

name.description: Tool identifier — must match either a skills[].id or mcpServers[].id for cross-referencing.

type.description: Whether the tool is a local skill or exposed via an MCP server.

trustBreakdown.description: Line-item factors that contribute to the trust score.

label.description: Human-readable explanation of the trust factor (e.g. 'High external tool usage'). Avoid opaque identifiers like 'dangerous_keyword:rm' — expand to 'Dangerous keyword detected: rm command'.

delta.description: Signed integer adjustment to the trust score. Negative = risk factor, positive = trust signal.

agenticApps.description: Every multi-agent application or workflow discovered on the endpoint.

id.description: Globally unique identifier for this app. Use '<sourceFilePath>' or a deterministic UUID.

name.description: Human-readable display name. Derive from the project directory + file (e.g. 'zeroclaw/Dockerfile', 'ah-verify/docker-compose'). MUST NOT be a bare generic like 'dockerfile' — always include the project context.

sourceFilePath.description: Absolute path to the file that defines or anchors this app (e.g. '/Users/will/project/docker-compose.yml'). This replaces embedding the path inside the 'description' field.

framework.description: Orchestration framework (e.g. 'LangGraph', 'CrewAI', 'AutoGen', 'Custom'). Detect from imports, config files, or framework-specific manifests.

agentCount.description: Number of distinct agents participating in this app.

reviewStatus.description: Whether a human has reviewed this app's configuration.

description.description: One-line functional summary of what the app does. Do NOT embed the file path here — use 'sourceFilePath' for that. Focus on behavior (e.g. 'Multi-agent code review system with linting, security checks, and style feedback').

agents.description: The ordered list of agents participating in this app. Each MUST reference the agent by its unique 'id' from the agents[] array. Index aligns with toolsByAgent.

toolsByAgent.description: Tools grouped by agent. toolsByAgent[i] corresponds to agents[i].

items.description: List of tool names available to this agent.

workflow.description: Ordered steps in the app's execution pipeline. If the pipeline cannot be statically determined, this may be empty.

agent.description: Display name of the agent performing this step.

action.description: Description of what happens in this step.

integrations.description: External systems the app connects to.

verificationChecks.description: Checklist labels the scanner evaluated. The dashboard derives pass/warn/fail from this list plus risk and reviewStatus.

riskTags.description: Short risk labels surfaced in the UI (e.g. 'Autonomous Code Execution', 'No Sandbox Isolation').

riskSummary.description: Paragraph-length risk narrative for this app. Do NOT embed absolute file paths — reference the app by name.
